reforms.description: Germany implemented an accelerated depreciation allowance for newly-founded enterprises to facilitate their establishment and growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allowance incentivizes investment in new enterprises, promoting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect as of 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: While beneficial, the reform is a minor adjustment rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Accelerated depreciation allowance for newly-founded enterprises.

reforms.description: The government plans to allocate a significant portion of the interest savings from the UMTS license sales towards projects crucial for maintaining and enhancing the economy's growth potential, specifically in infrastructure, education, and R&D. Additionally, the Federal and state governments established several support programmes for private enterprises in the new Länder, aimed at fostering investment, business start-ups, research and development, and exports.
reforms.component_name: UMTS License Savings Allocation and Support Programmes
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allocation of funds and establishment of support programmes are designed to enhance R&D and investment, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in funding allocation for R&D and support for private enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: To foster the reconstruction of the eastern German economy, beginning in 1990 the Federal Government and the governments of the new states as well as state-owned banks and the EU set up several support programmes for private enterprises, designed to foster investment, business start-ups, research and development, and exports.

reforms.description: The Federal Government relaxed immigration laws and started issuing working permits for foreign computer experts to address the shortage of IT professionals, which was perceived to slow down economic activity and employment growth.
reforms.component_name: Relaxation of Immigration Laws for IT Professionals
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing the shortage of IT professionals, this reform aims to enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in addressing a critical skills gap but does not represent a fundamental overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: In autumn 2000 the Federal Government relaxed immigration laws and started issuing working permits ('green cards') for foreign computer experts.

reforms.description: The Federal Government plans to allocate 5% of the funds from a special scheme for reducing youth unemployment to additional mobility grants for apprentices seeking vocational training away from home. Additionally, the German Monopolies Commission proposed fundamental reforms to public universities, advocating for greater independence in introducing tuition fees and performance-based salary schemes for university teachers to improve education quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance the quality of education and vocational training, which are essential for building a skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been proposed and are pending implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reforms represent a significant structural change in the education system.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Government plans to spend 5 per cent of the funds allocated to the special scheme for the reduction of youth unemployment on additional mobility grants to apprentices who want to take up vocational training in enterprises away from home.
